mnemonics: Justify, Analyze, Decide: Think of a Business Case as your project's J.A.D. to avoid getting S.A.D. (Stuck After Delivery).
keyPoints: A business case justifies the project by analyzing costs, benefits, and risks.
It provides the rationale for initiating a project and is used for decision-making and securing funding.
A strong business case aligns the project with strategic organizational goals and includes a recommended option.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on technical benefits while ignoring financial and strategic value.
Failing to update the business case throughout the project lifecycle to reflect changes.
Underestimating costs or overestimating benefits to make the project appear more favorable.
